fields.description:
## Why

The 2026-07-08 principal-architect audit (Reliability finding #3) found that three documented reconciler safety mechanisms are implemented, tested, and **never called in production** â€” the safety they promise is illusory, and the current epic's children do not cover them:

1. **Createâ†’label crash window double-creates Jira issues.** `dispatch_one.py:241-256` runs `create_issue` â†’ then adds the `rebar-id:` label the JQL dedup keys on â†’ then binds in memory only; bindings persist only at end of pass (`reconcile.py:1068`). A hard crash between create and label leaves an orphan issue invisible to dedup; the re-run creates a duplicate.
2. **The write-ahead/recovery protocol is dead code â€” and miswired.** `binding_store.py:6-16` documents pendingâ†’confirm; `bind_pending` has zero production callers, and `run_differs.py:267` passes the applier module (which has no `search_issues`) as the client to `recover_pending_bindings`, so real recovery would `AttributeError` into a fail-open swallow (`run_differs.py:268-274`).
3. **Crashed pass deadlocks reconciliation forever.** Lease `steal()` is implemented and tested (`_ref_lock.py:470-533`) but the held-ref path unconditionally exits 3 (`__main__.py:534-539`); after a SIGKILL the remote `refs/reconciler/lock` persists until an operator hand-deletes it.
4. **All three retry layers are inert.** The REST helper re-raises every `HTTPError` (`acli_rest.py:61-63`); `_call_with_backoff` (`acli_subprocess.py:236-292`) has zero production call sites; the applier's `_call_with_retry` only retries `JiraAPIError`, which nothing in the client layer raises (`dispatch_one.py:100-144`). Sibling C4 (lambswool-shaded-eider) fixes only the bulk-path 429 parity â€” it does not revive these layers.

## What

Wire each mechanism into its intended call site, smallest-change-first:

1. **Revive the retry layer WITHOUT a boundary translation.** Do NOT translate `HTTPError`â†’`JiraAPIError` at
   `acli_rest.py` â€” that would silently break the 8+ callers that catch raw `urllib.error.HTTPError` from the REST
   path (outbound_differ.py:134, apply_handlers.py:147/151 â€” which explicitly needs raw 404, acli_graph.py:259/360/454,
   fetcher.py:445/491/528, inbound_probe.py:107, acli_cli_ops.py:136, dispatch_one.py:484, applier.py:752). Instead,
   extend the applier's `_call_with_retry` (dispatch_one.py:100-144) to ALSO recognize a retryable
   `urllib.error.HTTPError` (429 with `Retry-After`; 5xx with jittered backoff) in addition to `JiraAPIError`,
   leaving acli_rest's raw-`HTTPError` raise UNCHANGED. Non-retryable statuses (4xx except 429) propagate raw exactly
   as today. This revives retry/backoff for the wrapped write path with ZERO blast radius on the raw-HTTPError
   callers. (`_call_with_backoff` in acli_subprocess.py stays out of scope â€” C4 owns the bulk 429 path.)
2. **Write-safety via a per-retry dedup guard (not generic re-invoke).** `_call_with_retry` re-invokes the same
   callable; the JQL dedup search (dispatch_one.py:196) runs ONCE before the wrapper, so a mid-flight 429/5xx on
   `create_issue`/`add_comment` could re-send and double-create. Wrap each non-idempotent op in a dedup-aware
   callable that RE-RUNS the rebar-id JQL search (create) / comment-marker check (comment) at the START of every
   retry attempt and short-circuits to the existing issue/comment if the dedup key now exists â€” so a retry after a
   possibly-succeeded write never duplicates. Idempotent ops (update/transition/label) retry directly.
3. **Write-ahead recovery.** Call `bind_pending` + persist the binding store **before** `create_issue`, confirm
   after label attach; fix `recover_pending_bindings` (called at `run_differs.py:351`) â€” it currently passes the
   `applier` MODULE (no `search_issues`) â†’ `AttributeError` into a fail-open swallow. Pass a real client (the
   AcliClient exposing `search_issues`, which `recover_pending_bindings` at binding_store.py:438 calls with the
   colon/hyphen rebar-id label). Make recovery failures LOUD: emit a **bridge alert via the existing `alert_store`
   channel** (the same fail-open, 24h-deduped path `run_differs._emit_outbound_field_alerts` uses) AND set a
   **nonzero `recovery_failures` manifest tally** â€” instead of the silent swallow.
4. **Crashed-pass lease steal.** In `__main__.py`'s held-lock path (`__main__.py:534-539`), invoke `steal()` when
   the lease is EXPIRED (fence semantics already implemented at `_ref_lock.py:470-533`); keep exit 3 for a live,
   unexpired holder.

## Scope

In: `dispatch_one.py` (`_call_with_retry` HTTPError-aware + dedup-guarded write callables + bind_pending-before-create),
`run_differs.py` (recover_pending_bindings client fix + loud failures), `__main__.py` (lock-path steal), plus tests
under `tests/scripts/bridge/` and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`. Read-only (relied on, not changed): `acli_rest.py`
(raw HTTPError raise UNCHANGED â€” verified non-breaking for its 8+ catchers), `binding_store.py` (`bind_pending`/
`recover_pending_bindings` already implemented). Out: bulk-path 429 parity (C4 owns it), inbound status mapping
(audit-remediation epic), any refactor of the retry helpers' structure, `_call_with_backoff` revival (C4).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_call_with_retry` retries a retryable `HTTPError`: 429 honoring `Retry-After` when the header is PRESENT and falling back to jittered exponential backoff when it is ABSENT (Jira Cloud does not guarantee the header â€” ADR 0036); 5xx with jittered backoff; non-retryable 4xx re-raised. WITHOUT translating at `acli_rest.py` â€” a test asserts the raw `HTTPError` raise from `acli_rest` is unchanged so the 8+ raw-`HTTPError` catchers (e.g. apply_handlers.py:151's 404 path) still see raw `HTTPError`. Both the header-present and header-absent 429 paths are tested.
- [ ] Non-idempotent writes (create/comment) re-run their dedup key check (rebar-id JQL / comment marker) at the START of every retry attempt and short-circuit if the key now exists â€” a test injects a 429 AFTER a create that already landed and asserts NO duplicate issue is created.
- [ ] `bind_pending` is written and the store persisted before `create_issue`; a crash injected between create and label leaves a pending record that the next pass recovers via a working `search_issues` client (no duplicate issue)
- [ ] `recover_pending_bindings` receives a client object exposing `search_issues` (not the applier module); a recovery failure emits a bridge alert via the `alert_store` channel AND sets a nonzero `recovery_failures` manifest tally, instead of the silent fail-open swallow (asserted by a test).
- [ ] A pass encountering an expired lease steals it via `steal()` and proceeds; a live unexpired lease still exits 3 (both covered by tests)
- [ ] Both test locations green: `python -m pytest tests/scripts/bridge/ -q` AND `python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ -q` (the new unit tests for the retry/dedup/recovery/steal behaviors land under `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`).
